decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since the heat spreader conductivity is already at its maximum bound (500.0), the next most effective lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve vertical heat extraction towards the cooled boundaries. This change stays within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Doubling base conductivity improves heat sinking capacity from the chip through the base to the Dirichlet boundaries, directly addressing the high temperature violation.

expected_effects: Improved thermal gradient across the base plate
risk_notes: Diminishing returns if base resistance is not the dominant bottleneck compared to contact or spreader resistance
May require subsequent geometry adjustments if material tuning alone is insufficient
